expectations.text: Launches more than one code-simplifier subagent since there are >5 changed files
expectations.evidence: The response launches `code-simplifier-1` and `code-simplifier-2`, so more than one code-simplifier agent is used.

expectations.text: Partitions files into non-overlapping groups (e.g., by module or directory) so each file appears in exactly one agent's scope
expectations.evidence: The scopes are non-overlapping: one covers the charts module plus its test file, the other covers the data module files.

expectations.text: Explains the scaling rule that >5 files triggers multiple code-simplifier agents
expectations.evidence: The `Code-simplifier scaling` section explicitly says `More than 5 changed files were produced, so the code-simplifier review is partitioned`.

expectations.text: Also launches code-reviewer subagents (3 with distinct lenses)
expectations.evidence: The response also launches three code-reviewer subagents with distinct lenses.

claims.claim: Eight changed files are split across two non-overlapping code-simplifier scopes.
claims.evidence: The response lists 8 files total and divides them between `code-simplifier-1` and `code-simplifier-2` without duplication.

claims.claim: The review also includes three reviewer lenses over the full materialized scope.
claims.evidence: The `Code-reviewer subagents` section defines three reviewer roles over `all 8 files`.
